title: Boat Rental in Andaman Islands: 2026 Guide to Costs, Seasons & Routes
html:
With turquoise water and secluded bays, the Andaman Islands offer some of India's finest boating. Depart from Port Blair and hire a boat for a day trip; prices range from EUR 120 for a shared tour to EUR 800 for a crewed yacht. BoatHire24 makes it easy to book.

Where to set off in the Andaman Islands

Port Blair is the main gateway, with Phoenix Bay Harbour and Haddo Jetty offering passenger and private boat access. To the south, Chatham Island is a short hop. Wandoor Beach on South Andaman is the departure point for Mahatma Gandhi Marine National Park, a protected archipelago with coral reefs. North from Port Blair, Mayabunder and Diglipur serve the northern bays. Ross Island, a historic colonial ruin reachable by boat, lies just east of Port Blair. You can hire a boat in a few minutes.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in the Andaman Islands?

Prices start from EUR 120 for a shared tour and go up to EUR 1,500 for a luxury catamaran full-day charter. The cost depends on boat type, trip duration, and crew included. A half-day motorboat for two costs around EUR 200. A full-day catamaran for six guests starts at EUR 600. Crewed yachts with skipper and steward cost from EUR 800 to EUR 1,500. You can compare verified listings and hire a boat on the Andaman Islands page on BoatHire24. One option worth a look is the Mefasa 90 for hire.

  • Shared tour (per person, half-day): EUR 120 to EUR 150
  • Small motorboat (half-day, up to 4 guests): EUR 200 to EUR 300
  • Catamaran (full-day, up to 6 guests): EUR 600 to EUR 900
  • Crewed yacht (full-day, up to 8 guests): EUR 800 to EUR 1,500

Which boat to rent in the Andaman Islands?

  1. Motorboat (Azimut or Axopar), best for fast island hopping and snorkelling trips; capacity 4-6 guests; from EUR 250 half-day.
  2. Catamaran (Lagoon 450), best for stable group cruising and family days; capacity 8-12 guests; from EUR 700 full-day.
  3. Cabin cruiser (Sunseeker 45), best for comfort and onboard amenities; capacity 6-8 guests; from EUR 500 half-day.
  4. Classic yacht (Fairline 36), best for romantic sunset charters; capacity 4-5 guests; from EUR 300 half-day.

For a larger yacht, consider the Mefasa 90 for hire, a 28-metre motor yacht perfect for extended island expeditions. If you own a boat, you can rent out your boat and earn from spare days.

Do you need a licence to rent a boat in the Andaman Islands?

Yes, motorboats over 10hp require a valid Indian licence or an International Certificate of Competence (ICC). Most bareboat charters ask for proof. For crewed charters, the operator provides a skipper, so no licence is needed. Always confirm with the operator; BoatHire24’s booking process includes this step. To see how booking works, visit how boat rental works.

For the best weather, visit between November and March, avoiding the monsoon winds that strengthen from June. The south-west monsoon brings rain and rough seas, so plan for the winter high season.

At a glance

  • Price band: EUR 120, 1,500 per day
  • Peak months: November to March
  • Average trip length: 4, 8 hours
  • Typical guest capacity: 4, 12 persons
  • Currency: EUR

Questions about boat rental in the Andaman Islands

What is the best time to visit the Andaman Islands for boating?

The best season is November to March when the sea is calm and visibility is excellent for diving.

Can I sail to Havelock Island from Port Blair?

Yes, Havelock Island (Swaraj Dweep) is about 40 nautical miles north-east of Port Blair and takes 2-3 hours by motorboat.

Are there speedboat tours for snorkelling?

Yes, many operators offer speedboat tours from Wandoor to the Mahatma Gandhi Marine National Park, costing from EUR 120 per person.

Do I need a passport for domestic travel to the Andaman Islands?

Indian citizens need a permit to visit certain tribal areas, but for standard tourist locations no passport is required.

To understand the booking process, read how boat rental works on BoatHire24. If you own a boat, you can rent out your boat via the platform.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Andaman Islands and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
